description: Post-audit sanitation contract. Ensures workspace cleanliness before certification and push. Runs ONLY after Auditor issues PASS. Any BLOCKING rule failure prevents certification from proceeding.
execution_order: Runs after Auditor PASS, before Signature Engine certification.

rules.name: git_status_clean_except_allowed_paths
rules.description: git status must show no modified, staged, or unstaged changes outside of WORK_PACKET.allowed_paths. Changes within allowed_paths are expected. Any diff outside allowed_paths indicates scope leak.

rules.name: no_untracked_files
rules.description: No untracked files may exist in the workspace that are not declared in WORK_PACKET.allowed_paths or standard artifact output paths (changesets/, audit_reports/, .garage/). Untracked files indicate leftover scratch work or accidental generation.

rules.name: no_temp_or_backup_files
rules.description: No files matching temp/backup patterns: *.tmp, *.bak, *.swp, *.swo, *~, *.orig, *.rej, .DS_Store, Thumbs.db, *.pyc, __pycache__/, .env, .env.local. Workspace must be production-clean.

rules.name: no_debug_statements
rules.description: Scan modified files for common debug artifacts: console.log (JS/TS), print() used as debug (Python), debugger statements, TODO/FIXME/HACK comments added in this execution. Existing debug statements in untouched code are not flagged.

rules.name: artifact_directories_complete
rules.description: All expected artifact directories must exist and contain their required files. changesets/outbox/<work_packet_id>/changeset.json must exist. Lane-specific subdirectories (db/, ui/, container/) must exist when their respective lane was active. Empty artifact directories indicate incomplete execution.

rules.name: no_modified_registry_files
rules.description: No files in law/registry/ may have been modified during Worker execution. Registry files are Garage-side governance. Any modification in the mounted child repo workspace is a governance leak. This is a redundant safety check complementing RULE-009.

rules.name: no_new_top_level_directories
rules.description: No new top-level directories may have been created in the workspace that are not part of the existing CTB branch structure or approved support surfaces. Prevents accidental directory sprawl.

rules.name: no_leftover_mount_directories
rules.description: No /workspaces/ mount remnants may exist after execution. Mount workspaces are ephemeral and must be cleaned up by the execution runner before sanitation runs. Leftover mounts indicate cleanup failure.

rules.name: no_uncommitted_generated_files
rules.description: All generated files (artifacts, changesets, audit reports) must be committed to the PR branch before sanitation completes. Uncommitted generated files will be lost on push and break the certification hash chain.

execution_protocol.scope: Mounted workspace only. Sanitation does not evaluate Garage-side files.
sequence: 2. Run all SAN rules against the mounted workspace.
3. Collect results: each rule emits PASS, FAIL, or WARN.
4. If any BLOCKING rule is FAIL: sanitation_status = FAIL. Certification blocked.
5. If only WARNING rules triggered: sanitation_status = PASS_WITH_WARNINGS. Certification proceeds. Warnings logged.
6. If all rules PASS: sanitation_status = CLEAN. Certification proceeds.

fields.work_packet_id: ID of the evaluated work packet.
fields.rules_evaluated: Array of { rule_id, name, result: PASS|FAIL|WARN, detail }.

fail_closed_rules: If sanitation_status === FAIL: certification MUST NOT proceed.
If sanitation_report is missing: certification MUST NOT proceed.
Sanitation cannot downgrade a BLOCKING FAIL to a WARNING.
Sanitation cannot modify any file. It is read-only evaluation.
Sanitation failures require a new WORK_PACKET to remediate and re-run the pipeline.
